payload.entry: Confirmation entry (Zero's two scenarios): (1) SAFER — break of the 'flip-high' (the value-area high it was rejected from) with a body close ABOVE, then a retest → long; (2) AGGRESSIVE — a sweep of the Value-Area-Low ~4,300, then reclaim/acceptance back above, push into demand, break the high, retest. If neither sets up cleanly: skip.
payload.invalidation: An accepted close BELOW the HTF demand zone (price has held above it — 'we also didn't close below this demand, very important; not even below the EQ'). A sweep of the VAL 4,300 that RECLAIMS keeps it valid; an accepted close below demand kills the long thesis.
payload.sharia_note: Gold is halal (a precious metal). Take SPOT via a PHYSICALLY-BACKED ETC — SGLN (iShares Physical Gold, GBP, no FX) / SGLD / IGLG (GBP-hedged). Zero's XAUUSD framing is a CFD — avoid gold CFDs / futures / leverage; own the physical-backed unit.
payload.note: Spot via physical ETC. HTF demand test he'd been waiting for held with a strong impulse + reaction; 4H reads accumulatory (not distributive). COT: non-commercial longs reducing but net skew still long → favours the setup. Metals complex accumulatory with 3 macro lows tapped — a healthy backdrop. Setup VALID but untriggered — awaiting the break+retest or the VAL-sweep+reclaim.
payload.caption: Gold — HTF demand reclaim; long on break+retest of the flip-high OR a VAL ~4,300 sweep+reclaim. Targets 4,800 / 5,000.
payload.text: Zero, mini market update (5 Jun, gold @19:31): demand test he'd flagged last update held with a strong impulse/reaction; price did NOT close below demand, not even below EQ. 4H = accumulatory. Setup valid. Two paths: (1) break the rejected value-area high with a body close above + retest → 4,800/5,000 ('easiest setup'); (2) given the accumulation, a sweep of the lows is possible — sweep VAL 4,300, reclaim, acceptance back, then long. Else skip. COT: non-commercial long contracts reducing but skew still long. All metals showing an accumulatory environment (3 macro lows tapped) — far healthier than distribution.
